suggestion: Install Ollama and pull required models for local AI inference
justification: Ollama provides failsafe AI inference when API keys are exhausted or unavailable

suggestion: Implement fallback provider selection when primary LLM fails
justification: System should gracefully degrade when primary LLM provider is unavailable

suggestion: Implement comprehensive input validation for API requests
justification: Prevent malicious or erroneous inputs from causing system failures and security vulnerabilities

suggestion: Add output validation checks to ensure expected formats and data types
justification: Ensure consistent, valid outputs from AI models and prevent unexpected behavior

suggestion: Enhance model input validation with specific checks for LLM providers
justification: Prevent incompatible or invalid inputs from causing failures in AI inference processes

Investigate DeepEval (https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval) as the LLM-application eval substrate for mindX
justification: mindX has no formal eval framework; the 0.2% Godel choice success rate cannot be improved without measurable scoring of relevance, hallucination, and task success. DeepEval is pytest-style and would let the autonomous improvement loop measure 'better' instead of 'different'. See data/config/library_registry.json#deepeval.
closed_by: agents/eval/ Phase 1 — GEval ported (Apache-2.0 fork) + log_godel_choice scoring

suggestion: Investigate Unsloth (https://unsloth.ai/) as the low-VRAM LoRA/QLoRA fine-tune pipeline once GPU budget exists
justification: mindX has no fine-tuning pipeline. Unsloth is the natural choice for resource-efficient tuning that can fold tuned weights back into the Ollama pillar via Modelfile-as-schema. Gated on GPU budget; document now so the path is known. See data/config/library_registry.json#unsloth.

suggestion: Phase 2: Replace hardcoded soldier-confidence ladder (0.3/0.5/0.6) in daio/governance/boardroom.py:259-275 with GEval scoring of (directive, vote, reasoning)
justification: Boardroom soldier confidence is currently a heuristic stamp, not a measurement. Calibrating with GEval (agents/eval/) will lift the consensus_alignment fitness axis (15% weight) onto a real signal. Use heuristic ladder as fallback. Vote-weight math unchanged. ~8 judge calls per session, CPU pillar by default.

suggestion: Phase 3: Add an 8th 'reasoning_quality' axis to the fitness leaderboard in mindx_backend_service/insight_aggregator.py — calibrated campaign-verdict scoring via GEval (agents/eval/)
justification: Today campaign verdict is string-match ('COMPLETED_GOAL_ACHIEVED'). Adding a calibrated reasoning_quality axis surfaces whether agents reason well, not only whether they emit the right success-string. Requires axis-weight rebalance to sum to 1.0; lift from latency_score / learning_velocity slightly. Phase 2 should ship first to validate the eval signal at scale.

suggestion: Install Ollama and pull required models for local AI inference
